summary: Matched `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim` in the Link Arrow item code and renamed the related `0x802A850C` helper from an address-style symbol to `itLinkArrow_802A850C`. The PR made small but important source-shape changes in `itlinkarrow.c`, updated the item header and symbol map, and adjusted Link/Kirby callsites to use the new symbol name.
what_changed: Renamed symbol `it_802A850C` to `itLinkArrow_802A850C` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, preserving address `.text:0x802A850C` and size `0x478`.
Updated the declaration in `src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.h` from `it_802A850C` to `itLinkArrow_802A850C`.
Updated Link's neutral special collision/end handling in `ftLk_SpecialN.c` to call `itLinkArrow_802A850C` instead of the old address-style name.
Updated Kirby's copied Link neutral special path in `ftKb_SpecialNPk.c` to call `itLinkArrow_802A850C`.
Renamed local inline helpers in `itlinkarrow.c` from `it_802A850C_inline` and `it_802A850C_inline_2` to `itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline` and `itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline_2`.
Adjusted local variable names in `itLinkArrow_802A850C`, including `var_r30` to `jobj` and `sp60` to `flags`, improving semantic clarity without changing the evidenced signature.
Changed `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline` to initialize `Item* item` and `HSD_JObj* jobj` at declaration time and to use `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` rather than directly reading `gobj->hsd_obj`.
Changed `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim` to use `GET_ITEM(gobj)` instead of direct `gobj->user_data` access.
Adjusted padding/stack-shaping declarations around `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim`, replacing `PAD_STACK(16)` with explicit small pad arrays in the shown diff.
smart_moves: Kept the behavior-facing callsites in Link and Kirby synchronized with the renamed item function, preventing header/symbol drift.
Used project accessor macros like `GET_ITEM` and `GET_JOBJ` in the matching target, which often helps express the same load pattern in a more canonical decomp style.
Renamed the exported function in symbols, header, implementation, and all visible callers in one PR, making the partially identified Link Arrow code easier to search and reuse.
Improved variable names only where the diff had clear semantic evidence, such as `jobj` for an `HSD_JObj*` and `flags` for the output parameter passed to `it_8026EA20`.
decomp_lessons: For item animation matches, small source-shape changes such as declaration placement, accessor macro choice, and explicit padding can be decisive without requiring algorithmic rewrites.

When a function remains partly unknown but is clearly item-specific, renaming from `it_802A850C` to a module-qualified name like `itLinkArrow_802A850C` improves navigability while preserving the address suffix.
Keep copied-character codepaths in sync with the source fighter's item helpers; Kirby's copied Link bow code needed the same function rename as Link's own neutral special.
Accessor macros such as `GET_ITEM(gobj)` and `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` can be preferable to direct field access when they produce the expected codegen and match local project style.
Stack padding may need to be represented as explicit local arrays rather than `PAD_STACK` depending on the target function's stack layout.
naming_conventions: Use `itLinkArrow_` prefix for identified Link Arrow item functions, even when retaining the address suffix for incompletely named behavior, as in `itLinkArrow_802A850C`.
Inline helpers tied to a named function followed the parent function name: `itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline` and `itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline_2`.
Existing animation function spelling in this slice is `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim` with lowercase `a` in `Linkarrow`; the PR did not normalize that spelling.
Semantic locals like `jobj`, `item`, and `flags` were favored over decompiler temporaries such as `var_r30` and `sp60` where type/use made meaning clear.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced direct `gobj->hsd_obj` access with `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` in the inline animation helper.
Moved declarations into initializer form for `Item* item = GET_ITEM(gobj);` and `HSD_JObj* jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj);`.
Changed padding representation near `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim` from `u8 _pad[4]; PAD_STACK(16);` to explicit `u8 pad[4];` and `u8 _pad[4];` in the shown source.
Adjusted line wrapping and expression formatting around pointer arithmetic such as `&item->xDD4_itemVar.linkarrow.x30 + i`, likely preserving source shape while satisfying style.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no comment body was included in the slice, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully name `itLinkArrow_802A850C` beyond the address suffix.
Search for additional Link Arrow animation functions using `GET_ITEM` or `GET_JOBJ` matching tactics.
Check whether `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim` spelling is later normalized to `itLinkArrow_UnkMotion1_Anim`.
Investigate nearby symbols `it_802A8330`, `it_802A8398`, `it_802A83E0`, and `it_802A8A7C` for further Link Arrow cleanup.
